items.title: The Illusion of Competition
items.authors: By Damian Fagon and Hedy Yang
items.summary: The Illusion of Competition maps every active cannabis brand in Illinois to the parent company that controls it — and finds market concentration rising again. That outcome was produced by policy choices: a licensing framework that gave incumbents a multiyear head start, regulators too fragmented to intervene, and a tax structure that hits small producers hardest.
items.imageAlt: Cover of The Illusion of Competition report

items.title: How to Federally Legalize Cannabis Without Violating the Constitution or Undermining Equity and Justice
items.summary: A blueprint for ending federal prohibition without violating the Constitution — protecting equity, small businesses, and the communities most harmed by the war on drugs.
items.imageAlt: Cover of How to Federally Legalize Cannabis report

items.title: Anti-Monopoly Toolkit
items.summary: A practical toolkit for legislators, regulators, patients, and advocates who want to stop big corporations from taking over the cannabis industry.
items.imageAlt: Cover of the Parabola Center Anti-Monopoly Toolkit

items.title: American Values and Beliefs on Marijuana Legalization
items.summary: Survey research on what American adults believe about who should shape marijuana policy — and who should benefit from legalization.
items.imageAlt: Cover of American Values and Beliefs on Marijuana Legalization
